stem: An engineer takes 30 measurements of tire pressure using a pressure gauge. The measurements have a mean of 181.4 psi and a standard deviation of 13.3 psi. However, calibration tests reveal that a pressure gauge systematically overestimates the true value by between 3.6% and 8.8%. Estimate the true tire pressure and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 181.4 psi. After correcting for the 6.2% bias: ≈ 170.8 psi. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (3.6%-8.8%) and sampling variability: [161.8, 180.0]. Point estimate: 170.8 psi.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
